title: Ebra

{{Quote|They are also used for patrols in areas with great altitude changes due to terrain, such as ''[…]'' the great canyons of Ebra.|Personal addendum by Airen Cracken, ''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide''|<ref name="CRFG">[[Cracken's Rebel Field Guide (sourcebook)|''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide'']]</ref>}}
'''Ebra''', also referred to as '''Ebran''', was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Kesh sector/Legends|Kesh sector]]. Its surface was [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] and featured [[Cave/Legends|caverns]] and large canyons such as the [[Grythsorin]]. Ebra was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Ebranite]]s, six-[[Limb/Legends|armed]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] who were well adapted for their environment. A relatively primitive, [[Thildas|clan]]-based [[Species/Legends|species]], the Ebranites exported the valuable [[Oil/Legends|oil]] [[lu-ramin]] off-world.

The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] subjugated Ebra, [[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] the Ebranites and forcing them to harvest lu-ramin. After the Ebranite sisters [[Rith Tar'ak]], [[Nyik]], and [[Tor'ara]] escaped Ebra and joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], hundreds of Ebranites became members of the Alliance in order to fight the Empire on Ebra. By [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], Ebra was a part of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==Description==
Ebra, also known as Ebran, was a rocky [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Dousc system]],<ref name="GG12" /> a part of the [[Kesh sector/Legends|Kesh sector]]<ref name="EAOC Dousc" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="TEA Dousc" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The planet occupied the second [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position in its [[Star system/Legends|star system]].<ref name="UAA" /> Four of the local seasons on Ebra were equivalent to three and a half [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]].<ref name="GG12" />

Ebra's surface was covered in<ref name="UAA" /> harsh<ref name="GG12" /> and towering<ref name="UAA" />—and seemingly endless<ref name="GG12" />—[[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] which were separated by very large canyons. Networks of [[Cave/Legends|caverns]] were also located on Ebra.<ref name="UAA" /> In the caves, supplies of pure [[Water/Legends|water]] supported an abundant population of [[Fungus/Legends|fungi]] and [[casanvine]]s.<ref name="GG12" /> The [[hag'thyyr]] was a [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Diet/Legends|predator]] native to Ebra.<ref name="CSWE hagthyyr" />

==History==
===Ebra and the Republic===
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
Around [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="date">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the mission "Meds for Vets" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the game, the mission must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> a local [[Stimulant/Legends|stimulant]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] was charging high prices for medicine to veterans located on Ebra. An individual affiliated with one of the sides in the ongoing struggle between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] sent a companion to Ebra, where the trader was subsequently persuaded to slightly lower [[Gender/Legends|his]] prices.<ref name="Meds for Vets">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Underworld Trading|Meds for Vets}}</ref>
{{Endgame}}

[[Millennium/Legends|Millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the society of the native [[Ebranite]]s was plagued with [[Thildas|clan]] warfare. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], however, the Ebranite clans adopted a peaceful and cooperative outlook, except for several clans, such as the [[Rull]] and the [[Jih-tan]], which retained their warrior way of life.<ref name="GG12" /> The Galactic Republic mostly did not interact with the Ebranites.<ref name="UAA" /> However, the natives did trade with the [[Carosite]]s,<ref name="GG12" /> a [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Carosus system]]<ref name="SW Planets">''[[The Star Wars Planets Collection]]''</ref> in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> and exported the valuable [[lu-ramin]] [[Oil/Legends|oil]], which was the primary component of the [[Foodstuff/Legends|beverage]] known as [[Ebran Lu-ramin Oilmead]].<ref name="GG12" />

===Ebra and the Empire===
[[File:RithTarak-Journal11.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Ebranite Rith Tar'ak was a native of Ebra.]]
During its early [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]],<ref name="CSWE Jenton">{{CSWECite|II|156|Jenton, Evitch}}</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] subjugated Ebra.<ref name="UAA" /> The Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]] [[Evitch Jenton]] assumed control over the planet<ref name="Journal11" /> and quickly instituted martial law.<ref name="CSWE Jenton" /> The Imperial government outlawed the Ebranite trade with the Carosites and restricted the lu-ramin export to numerous agricultural companies and cultures affiliated with the Empire.<ref name="GG12" /> Jenton also approved the [[Slavery/Legends|enslavement]] of Ebranites,<ref name="CSWE Jenton" /> and elements of the populace suspected of sedition or rebellious intentions were [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] to work in lu-ramin [[Mining/Legends|mines]].<ref name="UAA" />

At some point during the occupation of Ebra, the Rull clan of Ebranites carried out an [[Assault on the Imperial facility|assault]] on an Imperial facility on the planet. The superior [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] [[Death/Legends|slaughtered]] the attackers. The handful of Ebranite survivors of the raid, including [[Rith Tar'ak]] and her sisters [[Nyik]] and [[Tor'ara]], were taken [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] and forced to harvest lu-ramin in the nearby caves.<ref name="Journal11" />

===Ebra and the Rebellion===
Soon after their enslavement, Tar'ak led her sisters in a daring escape. The Ebranites captured an Imperial [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and forced him to transport them off Ebra. In a nearby system, the group was captured by a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. The Ebranite sisters joined the Alliance, eventually becoming a part of the elite Rebel unit known as the [[Scandium Team]].<ref name="Journal11" /> At some point, Ebra was mentioned in a personal addendum by Rebel [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] on a datapage of the ''[[Cracken's Rebel Field Guide]]'' detailing the [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]]-augmented [[R-444 Imperial Sky Swooper]] [[para-wing glider]].<ref name="CRFG" />

{{RPGseed}}
In [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' is set "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> a group of individuals affiliated with the Rebel Alliance visited Ebra. While in the planet's canyons, the group was engaged in a high-speed [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]] chase with Imperial [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout troopers]].<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref>
{{Endgame}}

Following her sisters' [[Wellte-ir Massacre|deaths]] in the line of duty, Rith Tar'ak eventually became one of the two Rebel agents tasked with recruiting and training the natives of Ebra.<ref name="Journal11" /> Eventually, hundreds of Ebranites joined the Rebellion in order to remove the Imperial presence from Ebra. A small percentage of the Ebranite population<ref name="GG12" /> began to explore the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]<ref name="UAA" /> between [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].<ref name="Dates Rebellion era">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' states the [[Ebranite]]s began leaving Ebra to explore [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the [[Rebellion era]], which {{Blog|holocron|24|Star Wars Eras}} dates to between [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].</ref> By [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], Ebra fell within the borders of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==Inhabitants==
===Ebranites and Imperials===
[[File:Ebranitegg12.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Ebra was the homeworld of the Ebranites.]]
Ebra was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Ebranites, a species of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], six-[[Limb/Legends|armed]]<ref name="UAA" /> omnivorous<ref name="GG12" /> [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. Adapted to their rocky home planet, the Ebranites were skilled climbers and [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] for food using [[Club/Legends|clubs]], [[Bow/Legends|bows]], and [[sling]]s. The primitive Ebranites organized themselves in clans named "thildas" and spoke the [[Ebranese]] [[Language/Legends|language]], and they were confined to their homeworld in the time leading up to<ref name="UAA" /> 0 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].<ref name="Dates Rebellion era" /> An exception to the technologically simplistic nature of the Ebranites was the hydro-thermal technology they utilized on Ebra.<ref name="GG12" />

The Imperial outpost on Ebra imported food and water in large quantities, and in return, the profitable planet exported lu-ramin as well as low-grade [[Ore/Legends|ores]].<ref name="GG12" /> The Imperial forces used R-444 Imperial Sky Swooper para-wing gliders for carrying out patrols in Ebra's canyons.<ref name="CRFG" />

===A droid for Ebra===
In order to facilitate the relations between the Empire and the Ebranites, the [[Technician/Legends|tech]] units that constituted a part of the Imperial occupation force on Ebra designed the [[5DS Ebranite Relations Droid]] by modifying the [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series protocol droid]]. However, the Empire did not realize that the creation of the new [[Protocol droid/Legends|droid]] actually served to further deepen the divide between the occupation forces and the Ebranites.<ref name="Journal11" />

The Ebranite Rebel agent Rith Tar'ak captured and [[Programmer/Legends|re-programmed]] the first 5DS [[Droid/Legends|droid]] produced, [[A-5DS]]. She proceeded to use the droid as a contact among the Ebranite settlements among the [[Grythsorin]] canyon. As a result of her species' distrust of high technology, Tar'ak was not fully confident in A-5DS and only used it when she was unable to visit Ebra in person.<ref name="Journal11" />

==Locations==
The Ebranite settlements,<ref name="UAA" /> known as clan warrens,<ref name="Journal11" /> were formed around small wells deep in the caves<ref name="GG12" /> of Ebra's canyons,<ref name="UAA" /> including the largest canyon, the Grythsorin. During its occupation of Ebra, the Galactic Empire established an outpost on the planet and operated lu-ramin mines in Grythsorin.<ref name="GG12" /> In addition, an Imperial facility was located near a set of Ebra caverns where a local strain of lu-ramin was grown.<ref name="Journal11" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ebra was first mentioned in the [[1991]] sourcebook [[Cracken's Rebel Field Guide (sourcebook)|''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide'']], which was written by [[Christopher Kubasik]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRFG" /> In the [[1995]] sourcebook ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', an adventure seed partially set on Ebra was included for use with the West End Games' [[roleplaying game]] system. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Dousc system, and therefore Ebra, in grid square L-6.<ref name="TEA Dousc" />
